channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Italy's Meloni, once Trump's closest ally in Europe, says he made up a story about her
item.description: "Italy and I do not beg," Meloni said in a video rebuke posted on social media Friday. Italy's top diplomat, meanwhile, said he was cancelling a visit to the U.S because of the alleged remarks.
item.content:encoded:
"Italy and I do not beg," Meloni said in a video rebuke posted on social media Friday. Italy's top diplomat, meanwhile, said he was cancelling a visit to the U.S because of the alleged remarks.

(Image credit: Geert Vanden Wijngaert)
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Rosman

item.title: US-Iran talks in Switzerland canceled. And, DHS to give police facial recognition app
item.description: The U.S.-Iran talks that were set to happen in Switzerland have been canceled. And, the Department of Homeland Security has plans to give some local police access to ICE facial recognition technology.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S.-Iran talks that were set to happen in Switzerland have been canceled. And, the Department of Homeland Security has plans to give some local police access to ICE facial recognition technology.

(Image credit: Ken Cedeno)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: What you need to know about the preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement signed by Trump
item.description: Here's a look at the preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran, and the challenges that remain to find lasting peace.
item.content:encoded:
Here's a look at the preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran, and the challenges that remain to find lasting peace.

(Image credit: Iranian Presidency Office via AP)
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Do Middle East nations still see the U.S. as a reliable ally?
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Dania Thafer of the Gulf International Forum whether countries in the Middle East consider the U.S. a reliable ally.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Dania Thafer of the Gulf International Forum whether countries in the Middle East consider the U.S. a reliable ally.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Labour's Andy Burnham wins a special election, setting up a showdown with Starmer to lead Britain
item.description: Labour's Andy Burnham, the current mayor of Greater Manchester, has won a special election for a seat in Parliament that puts him in a position to challenge embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer for leadership of the country.
item.content:encoded:
Labour's Andy Burnham, the current mayor of Greater Manchester, has won a special election for a seat in Parliament that puts him in a position to challenge embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer for leadership of the country.

(Image credit: Jon Super)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. strike on an alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean
item.description: The latest attack brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes by the U.S. military to at least 211 since the Trump administration began targeting those it calls "narcoterrorists" in early September.
item.content:encoded:
The latest attack brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes by the U.S. military to at least 211 since the Trump administration began targeting those it calls "narcoterrorists" in early September.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Secretary of Defense Hegseth scolds NATO allies — again
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth blasts NATO allies as a "paper tiger" while launching a six-month review of U.S. troop deployments in Europe and pressing for Europe to take the lead on its own defense.
item.content:encoded: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth blasts NATO allies as a "paper tiger" while launching a six-month review of U.S. troop deployments in Europe and pressing for Europe to take the lead on its own defense.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz

item.title: U.S. lifts blockade on Iranian ports as 60-day clock for a final deal starts ticking
item.description: The U.S. is allowing ships to enter and exit Iranian ports and coastal areas as the countries move to a new phase of negotiations over the next 60 days.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. is allowing ships to enter and exit Iranian ports and coastal areas as the countries move to a new phase of negotiations over the next 60 days.

(Image credit: Amirhossein Khorgooei)
item.dc:creator: NPR Politics and Policy Desk

item.title: Read the full text of Trump's preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement to end the war
item.description: Here is the text of the memorandum of understanding that was signed Wednesday by President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, as well as Pakistan's prime minister.
item.content:encoded:
Here is the text of the memorandum of understanding that was signed Wednesday by President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, as well as Pakistan's prime minister.

(Image credit: Nathan Laine)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack
item.description: Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday — one of the largest attacks on Russia's capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.
item.content:encoded:
Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday — one of the largest attacks on Russia's capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.

(Image credit: AFP)
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes
